action.reasoning: Observation: Best average_accuracy remains 0.4653 from iter 2 (memory=200/task, 8 epochs, Adam). Iter 7 with memory=400/task, 8 epochs achieved 0.4299 with val acc reaching 0.4316 progressively (early tasks ~0.31, late ~0.77). Training curves are broken (flat at loss 1.0, acc 0) due to insufficient or late logging. Analysis: The progressive val improvement shows learning is occurring but logging only captures end-of-run or scaled wrong. Forgetting is still present. Larger memory didn't help as much as expected possibly due to epochs or lr. Adding a final consolidation phase on the full memory buffer can help the model retain all classes better without new task bias. Decision: Update train.py to memory_per_task=350, epochs_per_task=6, lr=0.0008, add final 4-epoch training on memory only with lr=0.0002 after all tasks. Improve logging frequency (loss every 20 steps, val every 2 epochs). Complete and clean the memory update code for balanced sampling using meta. Then run training and submit_eval hoping for >0.47 avg_acc.
